description: Extend hex analyze to detect and report Rust workspace layers and boundary violations. Currently hex analyze only reports TypeScript src/ layer counts; 299 Rust files (87% of the codebase) are invisible to the health score and boundary checker.

specs.description: hex analyze reports Rust workspace layer counts when Cargo.toml is present
specs.given: Running `hex analyze .` in the hex-intf root (has Cargo.toml workspace)
specs.then: A 'Rust workspace layers' section appears showing Domain, Ports, Primary Adapters, Secondary Adapters, Use Cases with file counts sourced from Rust crate src/ directories

domain_conventions.layer_mapping: hex-core/src/domain → Domain, hex-core/src/ports → Ports, hex-nexus/src/adapters → Secondary Adapters, hex-nexus/src/orchestration → Use Cases, hex-nexus/src/routes + hex-cli/src/commands + hex-agent/src/adapters/primary → Primary Adapters

specs.description: Health score denominator includes all Rust source files
specs.given: The project has 452 source files (12 TS domain + 299 Rust + rest)
specs.then: The score denominator is the total file count including Rust files, not just TypeScript files

specs.description: Rust boundary violation detected when secondary adapter imports another secondary adapter
specs.given: A Rust file in hex-nexus/src/adapters/ contains `use crate::adapters::other_adapter`
specs.when: hex analyze runs the offline boundary scan
specs.then: A boundary violation is reported: 'secondary adapter imports secondary adapter'

domain_conventions.rule: Secondary adapters must only import from hex-core ports/domain, never from sibling adapters

specs.description: Domain layer violation detected when domain file imports from adapters
specs.given: A Rust file in hex-core/src/domain/ contains `use hex_nexus::adapters`
specs.when: hex analyze runs the offline boundary scan
specs.then: A boundary violation is reported: 'domain imports from adapters layer'
The JSON output contains a `rust_layers` array with `{ layer, file_count }` entries, parallel to the existing `layers` field for TypeScript

specs.description: Crates without recognized layer directories are counted as infrastructure
specs.given: hex-parser/ has no domain/, ports/, adapters/ subdirectories under src/
specs.then: hex-parser files are counted in total file count but reported as 'Infrastructure' — not penalized and not boundary-checked

specs.description: TypeScript-only project shows no Rust section
specs.given: Running hex analyze on a project with no Cargo.toml
specs.then: No 'Rust workspace layers' section appears; output is identical to current behavior

specs.description: Test-only use statements are excluded from boundary scan
specs.given: A Rust adapter file has `use other_adapter::Foo` inside `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { ... }`
specs.then: The use statement is not flagged as a violation — test code is excluded from boundary enforcement

specs.description: Clean hex-intf codebase produces zero Rust boundary violations
specs.given: The current hex-intf Rust codebase (which follows hex rules)
specs.when: hex analyze runs the Rust boundary scan
specs.then: Zero boundary violations are reported for the Rust workspace

specs.description: Rust layer display appears before the health score line
specs.given: Running `hex analyze .` with both TypeScript and Rust code present
specs.then: The display order is: Project structure → TypeScript hex layers (if src/ exists) → Rust workspace layers → boundary violations → health score
